How to Change Slack's UI Language

Quick note on changing Slack's UI language. Each Slack workspace has its own language setting, so the change applies per workspace — not globally across your Slack account. Useful if you want to switch a workspace into Traditional Chinese, Spanish, or any other supported language.

Seen people show off their GitHub profile pages and wondered how to do that? This post is the quick tour — creating the special username-named repo with a README that becomes your profile, plus using the github-readme-stats project to add stats cards, theme them, and show your top languages.
How to Get GitHub Pro for Free (via GitHub Student Developer Pack)

GitHub gives away a lot of free features, but Pro still has perks worth having — unlimited collaborators on private repos, branch protection rules, more GitHub Actions minutes. This post walks through getting GitHub Pro for free as a student via the GitHub Student Developer Pack — adding your school email to your GitHub account, applying, and waiting through the approval window.

CodePen is a great spot for frontend devs to put together quick demos — WYSIWYG online HTML/CSS/JS editor. This post is a quick intro for anyone who hasn't used it much — Pens vs. Projects vs. Collections, working with the editor, adding CDNs, preprocessors (Pug, Sass/SCSS/Less), formatting, Emmet customization, and the things that commonly trip people up (don't put `<body>` or `<head>` tags in your HTML pane).
Browser Forces Localhost to HTTPS? How to Clear HSTS in Chrome / Edge / Firefox

Web devs spend a lot of time on localhost and 127.0.0.1, but sometimes the browser forces HTTPS or throws "This site can't provide a secure connection" and "localhost refused to connect" errors. This post explains why — HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) — and how to clear it in Chrome, Edge, and Firefox.
Windows "running scripts is disabled on this system" — How to Fix PowerShell Execution Policy

Hit "running scripts is disabled on this system, so cannot load..." in Windows PowerShell? Windows defaults the Execution Policy to Restricted (especially on Windows 10 PowerShell), which blocks script execution as a security measure. This post explains the Execution Policy levels (AllSigned, Bypass, Default, RemoteSigned, Restricted, Undefined, Unrestricted) and how to switch to RemoteSigned so npm, vue, gulp, webpack, etc. work normally.
Why I Gave Up Evernote for Notion

Notion is a clean, capable note-taking app — clean enough that I dropped Evernote after almost five years on it. This post is about why I made the switch — Evernote's pricing/sync/Markdown gaps versus Notion's blocks, Markdown support, templates, workspaces, and the education plan that gives students Notion Personal for free.
How to Increase Your Google AdSense Revenue (Placement Tips)

A short notes post on the small things that move the needle on blog AdSense revenue — mostly about ad placement. Covers picking the right ad type (responsive Display ads), how many ads is too many, slots that are reliably visible (sidebar, article top, article bottom), and the two Google AdSense rules you should never break (no asking users to click, no clicking your own ads).
The Complete Mac Terminal Setup: iTerm2 + Zsh + Oh My Zsh + Powerlevel10k

A complete walkthrough of how I customize my Mac terminal — installing Homebrew, iTerm2, Zsh, Oh My Zsh, useful Zsh plugins (zsh-completions, zsh-autosuggestions, zsh-syntax-highlighting), themes (agnoster, Powerlevel10k), iTerm2 color schemes, and the edge cases that bite you (nvm, Homebrew PATH, VSCode font). Plus my full .zshrc for reference.
Notes on Switching From Windows 10 to macOS

Recently moved my dev environment to macOS. This post is a few notes on what surprised me in the Windows-to-macOS transition — modifier key remapping, input method switching, full-width vs. half-width characters, trackpad gesture differences, and the reversed scroll direction.
